defects.symptom: NO context subcommand answers --help/-h: `context equip --help`, `build --help`, `reconcile --help`, `council-batch --help`, `conventions-write --help`, `equip discover --help` all exit 2 with "unknown argument(s)"; `context hooks --help` errors "unknown hooks verb '--help'"; top-level `install --help` exits 2 with "unknown install argument"; `ingest --help` errors naming the wrong subcommand ("unknown argument(s) for `init`"). Hit in 12+ sessions; usage only discoverable by provoking errors. (Evidence items 5, 7, 10, 11, 13, 15, 16, 26, 27, 29, 40.)
defects.root_cause: The CLI is NOT argparse (evidence's mechanism guess is wrong) — it is a hand-rolled dispatcher. dispatch() in cli/__init__.py:96-98 handles -h/--help only when it is argv[0] (i.e. `dummyindex context --help`); for `context <sub> --help` the help token is passed through to the handler's run(argv), where every handler's leftover-args check rejects it with exit 2 (init.py:23, reconcile.py:32/60, build_loop/dispatch.py:86, council_batch.py:42, conventions.py:19, equip/dispatch.py:130, equip/discover.py:238) or misparses it as a verb (hooks.py:32). The sole exception is audit.run (audit.py:26-28) which checks args[:1] locally. Top-level: parse_install_args (installer/args.py:50-52) sys.exit(2)s on any unknown token including --help; __main__.py:244-247 rewrites `ingest` to dispatch(["init", ...]), so `ingest --help` produces an error naming `init`. All reproduced live at HEAD v0.25.0.
defects.proposed_fix: Central interception, one change point instead of 36 handlers: (1) In dispatch() (cli/__init__.py), after resolving `sub = ContextSubcommand(subcmd)`, check `if any(a in ('-h','--help') for a in rest): print(usage_for(sub)); return 0` BEFORE calling _HANDLERS[sub]. (2) Restructure cli/help.py's monolithic USAGE string into an ordered dict `SUBCOMMAND_USAGE: dict[str, str]` keyed by ContextSubcommand value (each value = that subcommand's existing block verbatim); assemble USAGE by joining, and add `usage_for(sub)` returning a `Usage: dummyindex context <sub> ...` header + the block. (3) Remove the now-dead local handling in audit.py:26-28. (4) In __main__.main(): for `install`/`uninstall`, check sys.argv[2:] for -h/--help before parse_install_args and print the install/uninstall section of _print_help, return 0; for `ingest`, intercept -h/--help and print the ingest section (also fixes the wrong-name error for the common case). Design decision: a literal '--help' could in theory be a VALUE of a value-taking flag (e.g. --note "--help"); recommend matching argparse semantics (intercept anywhere) for simplicity, or skip tokens directly following a _FLAGS_TAKING_VALUE member (cli/common.py:47-57) if strictness is wanted. Conventions respected: wire-only CLI modules, enum dispatch alphabet, print at the boundary, exit codes 0/2.
defects.tests_to_add: tests/cli/test_subcommand_help.py: parametrize over every ContextSubcommand member — dispatch([sub.value, '--help']) == 0, stdout contains sub.value and is non-empty, and (run in a tmp_path cwd via monkeypatch.chdir) NO file is created. Add cases: dispatch(['equip','discover','--help'])==0, dispatch(['hooks','--help'])==0, dispatch(['init','-h'])==0. Top-level tests in tests/test_install.py style: main() with argv ['dummyindex','install','--help'] exits 0 and prints usage (no install side effects); ['dummyindex','ingest','--help'] exits 0.

defects.symptom: Bare `dummyindex context equip` (no verb, no flags) executes a FULL APPLY — a help/discovery probe mutates repo state. Reproduced at HEAD in a fresh throwaway git repo with NO .context/: it wrote .claude/agents/generic-implementer.md, generic-tester.md, <proj>-reviewer.md, .claude/skills/<proj>-verify/SKILL.md and created .context/equipment.json (plus settings.json hook wiring when a formatter is detected). Similarly, bare `dummyindex install` runs the full install including the project init of cwd. (Evidence items 31, 15; pattern amplified by D1 since failed --help probes lead straight to bare-verb probes.)
defects.root_cause: _split_verb (equip/dispatch.py:105-117) deliberately defaults to EquipVerb.APPLY when the first token is not a verb — documented as 'back-compat with bare equip' — so `equip`, `equip <path>`, and `equip --anything-unknown-that-parses` all route to run_apply, which writes files, wires settings.json hooks, and writes the manifest with no confirmation and no .context/ prerequisite check. (`equip --help` itself exits 2 at dispatch.py:130 BEFORE writing — the mutation happens on the natural next probe, the bare verb.) Bare `dummyindex install` executes immediately at __main__.py:218-229; when cwd is a git repo it also runs the full project init (documented behavior, docs/guide/07-cli.md:22).
defects.proposed_fix: Make the destructive verb explicit: change _split_verb to return `Optional[EquipVerb]` — when args is empty or the first token is not an EquipVerb member, run() prints the equip usage section (from D1's usage_for) to stderr and returns 2 instead of defaulting to APPLY. Design decisions for the implementer: (a) back-compat carve-out — `equip --dry-run` (read-only, documented at skills/equip/SKILL.md:75) may be allowed to keep routing to apply-dry-run since it writes nothing; recommended rule: verbless invocation is allowed ONLY when --dry-run is present, otherwise exit 2 with 'equip now requires an explicit verb; did you mean `equip apply`?'. (b) Update every shipped caller and doc that relies on bare equip: skills/equip/SKILL.md:75-76 and :89 (`equip --specialist` → `equip apply --specialist`), skills/skill.md:335 prose, cli/help.py equip block (`equip [apply]` → `equip apply`), docs/guide/07-cli.md:298, and the equip/dispatch.py module docstring line 3. Additionally, run_apply should refuse (exit 1, clear message) when <root>/.context does not exist, since equip is documented as rendering FROM the .context spine — currently it half-initializes an un-indexed repo (creates .context/equipment.json from nothing). For `install`: keep semantics (it is the advertised one-shot bootstrap) — the D1 --help fix removes the probe hazard; no confirmation gate needed.
defects.tests_to_add: tests/cli/test_equip_verb_required.py: in tmp git repo, dispatch(['equip']) returns 2, prints usage, and creates NO files under .claude/ or .context/ (assert dirs absent); dispatch(['equip','--dry-run']) (if carve-out kept) returns 0 and writes nothing; dispatch(['equip','apply']) in a repo WITHOUT .context returns 1 with a 'run ingest first' message; existing equip apply tests updated to pass the explicit verb.

defects.symptom: `dummyindex context --help` is two releases behind the real surface: the equip block (help.py:252-298) lists apply/add-specialist/status/refresh/reset/uninstall/patch but NOT `discover`/`install` (both shipped, EquipVerb.DISCOVER/INSTALL); the build line (help.py:299) omits `--next-wave` (shipped, parsed at build_loop/dispatch.py:77-81); help says equipment.json '(v2)' while the CLI writes schema v3. docs/guide/07-cli.md has the same gaps (no equip discover/install sections, no --next-wave). equip/dispatch.py module docstring also says 'manifest v2' and omits discover/install from its verb list. (Evidence items 32, 39.)
defects.root_cause: USAGE is a hand-maintained monolithic string; the existing doc-sync guard (tests/cli/test_cli_doc_sync.py, added in 009f024 after the LAST such drift) asserts coverage only at ContextSubcommand-NAME granularity, so verbs and flags WITHIN a subcommand (equip discover/install, build --next-wave) and embedded literals like '(v2)' drift invisibly. SCHEMA_VERSION = 3 (constants.py:10) since the v0.19.0 plugin-manager work; help.py:255 still says '(v2)'. EquipVerb gained DISCOVER='discover' and INSTALL='install' (enums.py:40-41) with full handlers (equip/discover.py run_discover/run_install) but no usage lines.
defects.proposed_fix: (1) Text fixes: add two entries to the equip block in help.py — `equip discover ["query"] [--repo OWNER/NAME] [--root DIR] [--json]` (marketplace+GitHub plugin search matched to detected gaps; read-only) and `equip install <plugin>@<marketplace> [--yes] [--scope project|local|user] [--repo OWNER/NAME] [--usage-doc PATH|--skip-usage-doc] [--root DIR]` (flag surface confirmed at equip/discover.py:228-246 and :303-324); change build line to `(--next | --next-wave | --check "<item>" | --status)`; change '(v2)' to '(v3)' — or interpolate via lazy f-string from SCHEMA_VERSION; fix equip/dispatch.py docstring (v2→v3, add discover/install). Mirror all of it into docs/guide/07-cli.md. (2) Drift-proofing (the real fix): extend tests/cli/test_cli_doc_sync.py to verb/flag granularity — for every EquipVerb member assert `equip {verb.value}` (regex word-bounded) appears in dispatch(['--help']) output AND in docs/guide/07-cli.md; assert each of --next/--next-wave/--check/--status appears in the build usage block; assert f'(v{SCHEMA_VERSION})' (imported from the equip domain) is the version literal next to 'equipment.json' in USAGE. After D1's per-section restructure of help.py these assertions naturally target the right section.
defects.tests_to_add: Extend tests/cli/test_cli_doc_sync.py with: test_usage_documents_every_equip_verb (parametrize EquipVerb), test_usage_documents_build_verbs (--next, --next-wave, --check, --status), test_usage_equipment_schema_version_current (asserts f'(v{SCHEMA_VERSION})' in USAGE), and matching docs/guide/07-cli.md assertions.

defects.symptom: No read-only status entry point at any level: `dummyindex --status` → "error: unknown command '--status'" + full help dump (guessed 3x across sessions); `dummyindex context status` doesn't exist either. Models improvise flags because the only statuses are buried per-domain (equip status, build --proposal S --status, hooks status, config show, reconcile). (Evidence items 1, 19.)
defects.root_cause: ContextSubcommand (context/enums.py:32-75) has no STATUS member and __main__ has no top-level status command; the read-only building blocks all exist as separate domains (reconcile report = commit-anchored drift, equip classify_item, build --status, memory, config) but nothing composes them into one overview. The unknown-command path (__main__.py:249-251) dumps the full help with no nearest-command suggestion.
defects.proposed_fix: Add `context status` as a first-class read-only verb: new ContextSubcommand.STATUS + cli/status.py exporting run(argv) (registered in _HANDLERS), composing existing domains — .context presence + meta.json version/indexed_commit vs HEAD, drift summary counts (reuse the reconcile report domain read-only), equipment.json summary (items by state via classify_item, schema version), proposals with done/total (reuse build_loop status), pending-enrichment count, session-memory presence. Markdown to stdout + --json; exit 0 even when .context is missing (report 'not initialized'), per read-only contract. Add a top-level alias `dummyindex status` in __main__.main() delegating to dispatch(['status', ...]) exactly like the ingest→init alias (__main__.py:244-247 pattern), and document in _print_help. Design decisions: (a) verb name `status` (alias at top level recommended since both spellings were guessed); (b) exact JSON shape — recommend a flat envelope {indexed_commit, head, drifted, unassigned, awaiting_enrichment, equipment:{...}, proposals:[...]}. The new enum member automatically forces help.py + docs/guide/07-cli.md coverage via the existing doc-sync tests. Also append a suggestion line to the unknown-command error for tokens starting with '--' (e.g. "hint: flags go after a command; for an overview run `dummyindex status`").
defects.tests_to_add: tests/cli/test_status.py: status on an un-indexed tmp repo → exit 0, 'not initialized'; on a fixture .context → reports indexed_commit/drift/equipment/proposals; --json round-trips; asserts NOTHING is written (snapshot dir mtimes/file list). Doc-sync tests pass with the new enum member (forces help + guide updates).

defects.symptom: Terse mandatory-flag errors ("error: --from <id> and --into <id> are both required", "error: --feature <id> is required") carry no usage pointer, so agents probe by running verbs bare — which for equip/install EXECUTES (D2). (Evidence item 15 and the probing pattern across items 5/11/16.)
defects.root_cause: Each handler prints a one-line error and returns 2 with no pointer to fuller usage — and until D1 lands there IS no per-subcommand usage to point to. Confirmed live: `context features-merge` (bare) prints only the one-line requirement. The pattern is uniform across cli/features.py, build_loop/dispatch.py, audit.py, equip/*.
defects.proposed_fix: After D1: add a small helper in cli/common.py — `def usage_error(subcommand: str, message: str) -> int:` printing `error: {message}` then `hint: run \`dummyindex context {subcommand} --help\` for usage` to stderr, returning 2 — and adopt it at every exit-2 required-flag/unknown-argument site (the locations listed, plus the per-handler 'unknown argument(s)' sites from D1's location list). Purely additive to message text; exit codes unchanged. Depends on D1 (the hint must resolve).
defects.tests_to_add: tests/cli/test_usage_errors.py: parametrized — dispatch(['features-merge']) returns 2 and stderr contains both 'required' and '--help'; same for features-remove, build (no verb), scaffold-feature; assert no file writes.

defects.symptom: `equip apply` summary lies by omission: prints 'wrote 6 item(s)' while echoing only 4 `write` lines — manifest-only adoptions and settings.json hook wirings are counted as written but never echoed, while --dry-run DOES echo them; agents re-inspect equipment.json/settings.json by hand to learn what got wired. (Evidence item 2.)
defects.root_cause: _apply_write appends adopt items (dispatch.py:372-373) and hook record items (dispatch.py:386) to the same `written` list as file renders but prints no line for them, then the summary (dispatch.py:406-410) reports `wrote {len(written)} item(s)`. _apply_dry_run (dispatch.py:292-300) echoes adopt and hook lines, so the two renderers diverge — exactly the evidence's guess, confirmed.
defects.proposed_fix: In _apply_write: echo `  adopt   agent  {name}  ->  {path or '(registry specialist)'}` for each decision.adopt and `  hook    {name}  ->  .claude/settings.json ({event})` for each wired hook (mirroring _apply_dry_run's format, hooks echoed only when wire_hooks succeeded), and split the summary into honest buckets: `equip: wrote {n_files} file(s), adopted {n_adopt}, wired {n_hooks} hook(s), skipped ..., preserved ..., kept ... -> {path}`. Keep the JSON payload's `written` semantics (all manifest items) but consider adding `files`, `adopted`, `hooks` arrays for symmetry — design decision: keep `written` for back-compat and add the three subsets.
defects.tests_to_add: Extend tests for equip apply (tests/context mirror of cli/equip): fixture with a registry adoption + detected formatter — assert stdout contains one adopt line and one hook line, and that the summary's file count equals the number of `write` lines while adopt/hook counts are reported separately; JSON payload subsets sum to written.

defects.symptom: council-batch output (and --json) leaks the Python enum repr 'SubagentType.AI' as the agent name instead of 'AI Engineer', breaking the Task-tool dispatch contract the field exists for. (Evidence item 14.)
defects.root_cause: _dev_unit builds DispatchUnit with `subagent_type=str(pick.subagent_type)` (domains/council_batch.py:165). pick.subagent_type is a `(str, Enum)` member (DevPick.subagent_type: SubagentType, dev_pick.py:107) and `str()` on a str-mixin Enum returns 'SubagentType.AI', not the value — verified on the repo's Python 3.13: str(SubagentType.AI) == 'SubagentType.AI' while .value == 'AI Engineer'. The corrupted string then flows into both the human output (cli/council_batch.py:107) and DispatchUnit.to_dict --json (domains/council_batch.py:138). Note the PLAN/CRITIQUE branches are correct (they use .value / plain strings, lines 186, 199) — only the dev-unit path is wrong, which is why only stack-picked agents like AI Engineer leak.
defects.proposed_fix: One-line fix: domains/council_batch.py:165 `subagent_type=str(pick.subagent_type)` → `subagent_type=pick.subagent_type.value`. Hardening: DevPick.to_dict (dev_pick.py:111-117) currently emits raw enum members for subagent_type/fallbacks — json.dumps coerces str-subclass content correctly so `dev-pick --json` is unaffected, but emit `.value` explicitly there too so future f-string consumers can't regress.
defects.tests_to_add: tests/context/domains/test_council_batch_cli.py: add a case with a feature whose files match the AI/dev-pick rule — assert the dispatch unit's subagent_type == 'AI Engineer' (and --json output contains no 'SubagentType.' substring anywhere). Add a guard assertion: for every unit in a batch, 'SubagentType.' not in unit.subagent_type.

defects.symptom: `audit show --json` gives no way to locate the audit workspace (no dir key at all; report is null until written), forcing shell hacks to find .context/audits/<slug>/; `audit start --json` emits "dir": "audits/<slug>" which is relative to .context/ but doesn't say so. (Evidence item 28 — see not_confirmed for the literal 'dir: None' claim.)
defects.root_cause: The show --json payload (cli/audit.py:147-162) includes slug/mode/model/rounds/report but never the workspace directory; AuditStart.to_dict (models.py:109) hardcodes 'dir': f'audits/{slug}' (AUDITS_REL='audits', workspace.py:35) — a path relative to .context/ with no anchor, while the actual workspace is <root>/.context/audits/<slug>.
defects.proposed_fix: Add a 'dir' key to the show --json payload and normalize both start and show to the same repo-root-relative form: f'.context/{AUDITS_REL}/{cfg.slug}' (design decision: repo-root-relative recommended over absolute so output is machine-usable and stable across machines; if changing start's existing 'dir' shape is deemed breaking, add a new 'workspace' key to both instead and keep 'dir' as-is). Also include 'dir' in the non-JSON show output line.
defects.tests_to_add: tests for cli/audit show: after ensure_audit in tmp repo, `audit show --slug S --json` payload contains a dir/workspace key pointing at an existing directory when joined to the repo root; start and show emit the identical value.

defects.symptom: /dummyindex skill arg-routing treats CLI verbs as index scope paths: `/dummyindex usage chat` was interpreted as a bogus path, costing a filesystem probe + a user round-trip — and a less careful reading would have launched a full council re-index as the 'scope'. (Evidence item 0.)
defects.root_cause: The shipped skill's scope-resolution rules (skills/skill.md:14-19) instruct treating EVERY token after /dummyindex as a literal path (or failing with 'ambiguous scope'); there is no carve-out recognizing the CLI's own non-indexing top-level commands (usage, install, uninstall, context, ingest), which predate/postdate the rule.
defects.proposed_fix: Add a rule 0 to the scope-resolution list in skills/skill.md: 'If the first token is a dummyindex CLI command (usage, install, uninstall, context, ingest, status — i.e. anything `dummyindex --help` lists), the user wants that command: run `dummyindex <tokens>` verbatim, report its output, and STOP — never treat these tokens as an index scope and never start an ingest/council.' Keep the list in sync with __main__'s command set (include `status` once D4 lands). Markdown-only change; respects the 'routes via CLAUDE.md/SKILL.md, never intercepts' discipline.
defects.tests_to_add: Extend tests/cli/test_cli_doc_sync.py with a guard asserting skills/skill.md's routing block names every top-level command handled in __main__.main() (maintain the command list as a module constant in __main__ so the test can import it).

not_confirmed: Evidence mechanism 'argparse subparsers reject --help' — misdiagnosed mechanism: there is no argparse anywhere in this CLI; it is hand-rolled token-loop parsing (dummyindex/__main__.py + dummyindex/cli/*). The BEHAVIOR (exit 2 on --help) is fully confirmed and covered by defect 1; only the guessed cause was wrong.

Item 37 (`dummyindex --version` / `command -v dummyindex` empty in non-interactive shells, exit 1): not a code defect at HEAD — __main__.py:214-216 handles --version correctly and `python3 -m dummyindex --version` works. The transcript failures trace to PATH/uv-tool shim resolution in non-login shells (the known 'dummyindex CLI is a uv-tool COPY / snap PATH churn' install gotcha), outside this codebase.

Item 28's literal claim 'audit show --json returns dir: None': not reproducible at HEAD — `show --json` has NO dir key at all (cli/audit.py:147-162) and `start --json` always emits 'audits/<slug>' (models.py:109), never None. The substantive gap (workspace path not discoverable from show) is real and covered as defect 8.

Evidence items 3, 4, 6, 8, 9, 12, 17, 18, 20, 21, 22, 23, 24, 25, 30, 33, 34, 35, 36, 38, 41, 42 in this cluster file overlap other clusters (batch/bulk verbs, reconcile semantics, checklist/build-loop workflow, skill orchestration, gitignore hygiene) and were deliberately not investigated here per the cluster-focus rule — not 'unconfirmed', just out of C5 scope.
quick_wins: dummyindex/context/domains/council_batch.py:165 — change `str(pick.subagent_type)` to `pick.subagent_type.value` (1 line; kills the 'SubagentType.AI' leak in both human and --json output).
dummyindex/cli/help.py:255 — '(v2)' → '(v3)' next to equipment.json (1 word; the CLI writes SCHEMA_VERSION=3 per context/domains/equip/constants.py:10).
dummyindex/cli/help.py:299 — add `--next-wave` to the build verb line: `build --proposal S (--next | --next-wave | --check "<item>" | --status) [--json]` (1 line).
dummyindex/cli/equip/dispatch.py:3-17 — module docstring: add discover/install to the verb list, 'manifest v2' → 'manifest v3' (cosmetic, 3 lines).

dummyindex/installer/args.py — add an `elif a in ('-h','--help')` branch in parse_install_args that prints the install usage lines and sys.exit(0) (~6 lines; makes `dummyindex install --help` safe immediately even before the full D1 restructure).
dummyindex/cli/audit.py:149-160 — add `"dir": f".context/{AUDITS_REL}/{cfg.slug}"` to the show --json payload (1 line).
